Package javashooter.gameobjects

Class GameObject

java.lang.Object
javashooter.gameobjects.GameObject
Direct Known Subclasses:
AnimatedGameobject, BitmapObject, CircleObject, FallingStar, RectObject, TextObject
public class GameObject extends Object
The class GameObject represents an object appearing in a level of the game described by an instance of the class Playground. An instance of GameObject has the following properties:
  • a name that is unique within a certain Playground
  • 2D screen position x and y in pixels
  • 2D speed x and y in pixels/s
  • a reference to the Playground object it belongs to

In order to allow a flexible composition of object properties, an instance of GameObject is an aggregation of up to three instances of other classes. To put it another way, each instance has attributes that are references to:
  • a subclass of ObjectController that handles the movement/input logic of the object
  • a subclass of Collider that handles the collision detection of the object
  • a subclass of Artist that draws the object
When a GameObject is constructed using new, all of these references are set to null. In this state, the object does nothing, collides with nothing and is not visible because it is not drawn. You can use calls to the methods #setColliders, addArtist(javashooter.rendering.Artist), addCollider(javashooter.collider.Collider) and setController(javashooter.controller.ObjectController) to set these references later, thus giving to an object a behavior, the ability to collide with others, and a visual appearance.
Normally, it is not necessary to create subclasses of GameObject since the composition of different behavior, collision and appearance properties is governed by subclassing the classes ObjectController, Collider and Artist and then adding them to the GameObject instance.
However, some subclasses of GameObject define convenience constructors where you can directly provide these references to have more readable code. Examples are: AnimatedGameobject, TextObject, RectObject and CircleObject. For now, only colliders that are centered on the object itself are supported.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameObject

      public GameObject(String id, Playground playground, ObjectController controller, double x, double y, double vx, double vy)
      convenience constructor.
      Parameters:
      id - Object name, must be unique
      javashooter - .playground Level this object should be part of
      javashooter - .controller Can be null if no javashooter.controller is needed
      x - X position
      y - Y poition
      vx - X speed
      vy - Y speed

    • GameObject

      public GameObject(String id, Playground playground, double x, double y, double vx, double vy)
      primary constructor. Intializes al aggregated instances to null, need to be set afterwards.
      Parameters:
      id - Object name, must be unique
      javashooter - .playground Level this object should be part of
      x - X position
      y - Y poition
      vx - X speed
      vy - Y speed

      public double getPhi()

    • setPhi

      public GameObject setPhi(double phi)

    • generateColliders

      public GameObject generateColliders()
      Can be overwritten in subclasses to generate specific colliders automatically. For example, a TextObject knows its own shape so it can generate the necessary rectangular javashooter.collider automatically.
      Returns:
      The instance of GameObject that is modified (i.e., this)

    • setController

      public GameObject setController(ObjectController c)

    • setObjectController

      public void setObjectController(ObjectController controller)
      Sets a new object javashooter.controller. Can be done anytime at all.
      Parameters:
      javashooter - .controller An instance of ObjectController or one of its subclasses.

    • getObjectController

      public ObjectController getObjectController()
      Returns ref 2 object javashooter.controller.
      Returns:
      Reference to ObjectController instance.

    • addArtist

      public GameObject addArtist(Artist a)
      set the Artist reference of this GmeObject.
      Parameters:
      a - This subclass of Artist
      Returns:
      this

    • addCollider

      public GameObject addCollider(Collider c)
      Add a javashooter.collider to the existing list of colliders. If it is null, create it and add the javashooter.collider anyway.
      Parameters:
      c - A subclass of Collider
      Returns:
      this

    • getPlayground

      public Playground getPlayground()

    • setPlayground

      public void setPlayground(Playground playground)

    • collisionDetection

      public boolean collisionDetection(GameObject other)
      Called by the game engine, DO NOT CALL YOURSELF. Is used to detect collision of this with another GameObject instance other. The task is delegated to the list of aggregated colliders. If there is no aggregated javashooter.collider, no collisions are reported.
      Parameters:
      other - , a GameObject procided by the game engine
      Returns:
      true or false, depending on whether this an other collide, as determined by both their colliders

    • getContactPoint

      public double[] getContactPoint()
      WHAT TO DO? Transform SPEED VECTOR to INTRINSIC CS. COMPUTE REFLECTED Vector, potentially based on contact point. transform back.

    • convertSpeedVectorBack

      public void convertSpeedVectorBack(double[] speedVec, double[] dest)
      Convert a speed vector from the intrinsic CS of the collider that had the last coll for this object, back to the global CS
      Parameters:
      speedVec - 2-Vector of speed in global CS
      dest - sppedVec in intrinsic CS

    • convertSpeedVector

      public void convertSpeedVector(double[] speedVec, double[] dest)
      Convert a speed vector to the CS of the collider that had the last coll for this object.
      Parameters:
      speedVec - 2-Vector of speed in global CS
      dest - sppedVec in intrinsic CS

    • updateObject

      public void updateObject()
      Is called by the game engine once per time step, DO NOT CALL YOURSELF. Gives an object the chance to react to the current game state, change its position and speed a.s.o, react to user inputs, ... All that is done here is to delegate that task to the aggregated subclass of ObjectController. If that ref is null, nothing happens.

      public boolean isActive()

      public GameObject setActive(boolean flag)

      return the unique object ID.
      Returns:

    • setId

      public void setId(String s)

    • getGameTime

      public double getGameTime()
      Delegate gameTime computation to the Playground that a GameObject is part of.
      Returns:
      The game time in seconds

    • draw

      public void draw(Graphics2D g)
      Draws the object in its current state. Is called by the game engine, should NOT be called by the user. All it does it to delegate the task to the aggregated subclass of Artist. If this referene is null, nothing happens.
      Parameters:
      g - Graphics2D object that has all the necessary drawing functionalities

    • setComponentProperty

      public void setComponentProperty(String comp, String property, Object value)
      Experimental, use at your own risk. Uses reflection to call a particular setter method in one of the aggregated instances. Assuming that is exists of course. Duh.
      Parameters:
      comp - The aggregated instance to use. For now, only "javashooter.controller" is supported.
      property - The property to be set. Assumes that an appropriate setter exists.
      value - Value to be set.
